# ProJob Wiki Full Context

This file summarizes the current research wiki for LLM tools. The source of truth remains the Markdown documentation in this repository.

## Purpose

ProJob Wiki researches open-source options for a multi-functional, offline-first PWA solution for tradespeople and work-site projects. The platform scope includes checklists, job lists, resource management, scheduling, cross-project dependencies, quoting, field evidence, materials, stock, and cross-company collaboration.

The plain-English entry point is the homepage: https://ajdench.github.io/ProJob-Wiki/

The detailed research overview is: https://ajdench.github.io/ProJob-Wiki/research-overview/

The root design contract is: https://github.com/ajdench/ProJob-Wiki/blob/main/design.md

The first UI implementation spike is: https://github.com/ajdench/ProJob-Wiki/tree/main/experiments/projob-ui-spike

The current application suite blueprint is: https://ajdench.github.io/ProJob-Wiki/architecture/application-suite-blueprint/

The static UI demo page is: https://ajdench.github.io/ProJob-Wiki/demo/

## Key Architectural Position

No single open-source product appears to satisfy the full requirement set cleanly. The strongest direction is:

- ERP/FSM core for customers, sites, quotes, jobs, stock, invoices, approvals, and audit records.
- Dedicated offline-first PWA for field execution: assigned jobs, checklists, photos, signatures, time, materials, notes, and sync queue.
- Planning layer only where cross-project dependencies, Gantt, or programme reporting matter.
- Markdown/MkDocs wiki for human-readable requirements, research, architecture, ADRs, and proof-of-concept evidence.

## Architecture Additions

The wiki now includes a gap register and explicit stack architecture:

- Gap Register: records missing/thin areas, completed augmentations, and remaining POC evidence needs.
- Web and Stack Architecture: separates the current documentation stack, local POC stack, and future product stack.
- Suite Composition and Design: defines how upstream components should be synthesised into one ProJob application suite with shared navigation, terminology, styling, roles, and workflow surfaces.
- Application Suite Blueprint: maps ProJob workspaces, workflows, data objects, component patterns, and the first vertical slice into a buildable app-suite plan.
- Demo Architecture: records the static GitHub Pages demo boundary, Odoo/OpenProject fixture-adapter strategy, and JS tool bundling guidance.
- UI Framework Options: compares open-source UI framework and component-library families for aligned mobile, tablet, and desktop ProJob screens. Current first research direction is React + TypeScript + Tailwind + shadcn/ui/Radix + TanStack tools, with Quasar, Ionic, MUI, Mantine, Chakra, PrimeReact, Skeleton, and Shoelace/Web Components as comparisons.
- DESIGN.md Standard: explains how the root design contract is validated, used by prototypes, and kept aligned with wiki architecture.
- UI Framework Spike Findings: records the first React/Vite/Tailwind/shadcn implementation spike, including component ownership, responsive fit, offline/sync UI behavior, and browser verification notes.
- Component Map: maps ownership of customers, jobs, quotes, checklists, materials, files, dependencies, reporting, and audit across ERP/FSM, Field PWA, Sync API, file storage, and planning.
- Integration Contracts: defines canonical entities, field read APIs, mutation envelopes, attachment workflow, ERP adapter methods, planning adapter events, and conflict responses.
- Deployment Runtime: records GitHub Pages/MkDocs hosting, Docker/Colima local runtime, Odoo/OCA compose runtime, and staged future production deployment.

## Recommended Shortlist

Evaluate these first:

1. Odoo Community + OCA Field Service.
2. ERPNext / Frappe.
3. Custom offline-first PWA using RxDB or PouchDB/CouchDB.

Use Dolibarr, Atlas CMMS, openMAINT, OpenProject, and KoboToolbox/ODK as secondary benchmarks or companion systems.

## Core Requirements

- Jobs/work orders: create, assign, schedule, status-track, complete, reopen, and audit work.
- Checklists: templates per trade/job type, required evidence, pass/fail/N/A, signatures.
- Offline: field users can open assigned work, edit forms, attach evidence, and queue changes offline.
- Sync: conflict handling, retry queue, visible pending state, server-side audit log.
- Quoting: estimates, proposals, line items, alternates, variation quotes, approval workflows.
- Scheduling: calendar, job duration, technician/crew assignment, recurring work.
- Dependencies: internal and external predecessor/successor relationships and blocker visibility.
- Resources: people, skills, availability, vehicles, tools, equipment, materials, stock.
- Documents: site docs, drawings, method statements, risk assessments, versioned attachments.
- Collaboration: comments, mentions, external portal, subcontractor-scoped access.
- Permissions: role-based access plus company/project/site/job/document scoping.

## Application Suite Blueprint

The suite should be organised as role-aware workspaces in one shared shell:

- Field workspace for assigned jobs, offline checklists, evidence, time, materials, and sync queue.
- Supervisor workspace for review, approvals, exceptions, variations, and blockers.
- Scheduling workspace for crews, skills, vehicles, dates, dependencies, and planned materials.
- Estimating workspace for quotes, variations, approvals, and quote-to-job handoff.
- Resources workspace for people, skills, vehicles, tools, equipment, material availability, and stock movements.
- Projects workspace for milestones, blockers, dependencies, subcontractor tasks, and programme impact.
- Portal workspace for scoped client/subcontractor status, approvals, documents, evidence, and comments.
- Admin workspace for templates, roles, permissions, integrations, mappings, and audit exports.

The first vertical slice should prove: office assigns a job with checklist/materials; field user opens it online, works offline, records checklist answers, notes, material usage, time, photo, and signature; sync queue applies mutations when the network returns; supervisor reviews completion and raises a variation or approves invoice-ready completion. The UI spike now includes a mocked version of this loop with `localStorage`, offline mode, mutation queue states, sync, supervisor review, approval, and static Odoo/OpenProject-shaped source modes.

The shadcn MCP confirms the repo is configured for the `@shadcn` registry and exposes table/data-table and form/RHF examples that match ProJob's office and checklist surfaces. shadcn remains a primitive source; ProJob should own app shell, job cards, checklist rows, evidence panels, sync queue, review queues, scheduler views, and dependency views.

## Offline Acceptance Criteria

- Worker can start the PWA with no network and see previously assigned jobs.
- Worker can complete checklist, notes, photos, material usage, time, and signature offline.
- UI clearly shows unsynced changes and failed retries.
- Sync is idempotent and does not duplicate records or attachments.
- Conflicts produce reviewable states rather than silent overwrite.
- Office users can see sync status and last-known device state.

## Common POC Scenario

Every candidate should run the same scenario:

1. Create customer and site.
2. Create project.
3. Create quote with labour, materials, and optional variation line.
4. Approve quote and create job/work order.
5. Attach checklist and site documents.
6. Schedule job to worker and vehicle.
7. Assign planned materials.
8. Worker opens job while online.
9. Disable network.
10. Complete pre-work risk checklist.
11. Add job notes, time entry, used materials, photo evidence, and client signature.
12. Mark job complete offline.
13. Restore network.
14. Sync changes.
15. Supervisor reviews completion and flags a variation.
16. Create and approve variation quote.
17. Generate invoice or invoice-ready record.
18. Record dependency impact against subcontractor task.

## Candidate Roles

Odoo Community + OCA Field Service: strongest broad ERP/FSM option with field service modules, sales, stock, timesheets, vehicles, routes, agreements, and integrations. Likely system-of-record candidate, but offline PWA likely required.

ERPNext / Frappe: coherent open-source ERP and customization framework with selling, stock, projects, maintenance, workflows, APIs, and custom doctypes. Strong candidate if custom workflows are central.

Dolibarr: simpler SME ERP/CRM baseline with proposals, orders, projects, tasks, interventions, stock, agenda, billing, and APIs. Good lower-complexity benchmark.

OpenProject: best planning/programme layer for Gantt, dependencies, work packages, wiki, meetings, time, cost, and documents. Not a field execution tool.

Atlas CMMS: useful CMMS benchmark for work orders, preventive maintenance, assets, parts, mobile use, and scheduling. Not a complete quote-to-cash platform.

openMAINT: asset/facility maintenance benchmark with scheduled maintenance, work orders, asset movement, spare parts, purchase requests, contracts, and workflows.

Offline-first PWA stack: required product surface for low-connectivity field execution. Candidate stacks include PouchDB/CouchDB, RxDB, Dexie.js, ElectricSQL, and PowerSync.

KoboToolbox / ODK: strong offline forms/checklist/data-collection tools; useful as a subsystem or reference, not a complete business operating platform.

## Decision Records

- ADR 0001: Evaluate Odoo/OCA, ERPNext/Frappe, and custom offline-first PWA first.
- ADR 0002: Use Markdown plus MkDocs Material for human-readable internal documentation.
- ADR 0003: Use Jujutsu with colocated Git for local version control.
